Title: Duplicating Food Item
Description:
As an organization or location user 

I want to create and manage food lists 

So that I can organize food items, define temperature ranges, and assign them to locations


In Scope: Portal
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario: View 3-Dots Menu When Viewing/Editing Existing Food Item
  Given I am viewing or editing an existing food item in the side sheet with manage permission
  Then I see a 3-dots menu in the top right of the side sheet

Scenario: Duplicate Food Item from 3-Dots Menu
  Given I am in the side sheet viewing or editing an existing food item with manage permission
  If I click the 3-dots menu in the top right
  And I select the duplicate option
  Then the current side sheet closes
  And a new side sheet opens in create mode
  And all fields are pre-filled with the values from the original food item
  And the Food Name field is set to "ORIGINAL FOOD NAME - Copy"
  And the duplicated food is added to the Food Items table

Title: Duplicating Food List
Description:
As an organization or location user 

I want to create and manage food lists 

So that I can organize food items, define temperature ranges, and assign them to locations


In Scope: Portal
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario: View 3-Dots Menu on Food List Page
  Given I am viewing a food list details page
  Then I see a 3-dots menu in the top right

Scenario: Duplicate Food List - Organization User
  Given I am an organization member with permission to edit and copy any food list
  If I click the 3-dots menu in the top right
  And I select duplicate
  Then a new food list is created
  And the new food list title is "ORIGINAL FOOD LIST NAME - Copy"
  And all food items and their temperature ranges are copied over
  And I am navigated into the new duplicated food list

Scenario: Duplicate Food List - Location Manager
  Given I am a Location Manager with access to a food list
  If I click the 3-dots menu in the top right
  And I select duplicate
  Then a new food list is created (as a local food list if duplicating from org level)
  And the new food list title is "ORIGINAL FOOD LIST NAME - Copy"
  And all food items and their temperature ranges are copied over
  And I am navigated into the new duplicated food list
  # Note: This works even if I cannot edit the original food list
Scenario: Permissions - No Access to Duplicate
  Given I do not have permission to manage food lists
  Then the duplicate option is not visible or is disabled in the 3-dots menu

Title: Edit Food List Title
Description:
As an organization or location user 

I want to create and manage food lists 

So that I can organize food items, define temperature ranges, and assign them to locations


In Scope: Portal
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario: Edit Food List Title
  Given I have manage permission and am viewing a food list
  If I click the edit icon near the title, input a new name, and click save
  Then the title updates

Title: Food List: Create Food Item
Description:
As an organization or location user 

I want to create and manage food lists 

So that I can organize food items, define temperature ranges, and assign them to locations


In Scope: Portal

Design: https://www.figma.com/design/QbU1BEOWQfSc9LVrPYiJa2/Food-Lists?node-id=373-76705&m=dev
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1: Open Create Food Side Sheet
Given I am on the Food Items tab
If I click the create food button
Then a side sheet appears
And I see a food name field (required)
And I see a temperature unit selector (Default Fahrenheit)
  • Fahrenheit
  • Celcius  
And I see a temperature ranges section and a add range button for both (at least one is required)
  • Hot holding
  • Cold holding
And I see a cancel button and a X button that I can tap to exit the window
And I can click outside the window to exit the window
And I see a save button (inactive) that becomes active when I all required fields are entered 

Scenario 2: Add Hot Holding Temperature Range
  Given I am in the create food side sheet
  If I select "+ Hot Holding"
  Then compliant temperature range and preferred range appear with min and max values
  And minimum temp in compliant range defaults to 140F
  And the temperature gauge shows red for less than 140F and green for higher
  And at least one compliant value is required


Scenario 2: Add Cold Holding Temperature Range
Given I am in the create food side sheet
If I select "+ Cold Holding"
Then compliant temperature range and preferred range appear with min and max values
And maximum temp in compliant range defaults to 41F
And the temperature gauge shows red for greater than 41F and green for lower
And at least one compliant value is required
And the gauge adjust as I type I enter temperatures
And I see a remove button that I can click to remove the cold holding ranges


Scenario 3: Add Hot Holding Temperature Range
Given I am in the create food side sheet
If I select "+ Hot Holding"
Then compliant temperature range and preferred range appear with min and max values
And minimum temp in compliant range defaults to 140F
And the temperature gauge shows red for less than 140F and green for lower
And at least one compliant value is required
And the gauge adjust as I type I enter temperatures
And I see a remove button that I can click to remove the hot holding ranges 


Scenario 4: Add Preferred Temperature Range
Given I have added a compliant temp range in the side sheet
If I add a preferred temp range
Then the compliant area turns yellow in the temp gauge
And preferred becomes green
And values outside compliant or preferred are red
Scenario 5: Save Food Item
Given I am in the create food side sheet with required fields filled and one compliant temp added
If I click save
Then the side sheet closes
And the food appears in the Food Items table
And a toast confirms "Food item added to the list"
And the food item inherits a "Temperature" category


Scenario 6: Cancel Food Creation
Given I am in the create food side sheet
If I click exit without 
Then the side sheet closes without saving

Title: Food List: Create Food List Modal
Description:
As an organization or location user 

I want to create and manage food lists 

So that I can organize food items, define temperature ranges, and assign them to locations


In Scope: Portal

Design: https://www.figma.com/design/QbU1BEOWQfSc9LVrPYiJa2/Food-Lists?node-id=466-41136&m=dev
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1: Enter Food List Creation Modal
Given I am on the Food List page as a user with Manage Food Lists permission
If I click the Create Food List button
Then a modal appears to add Food List title (required) and subtitle (not required)
And I see a save button (default inactive)
And the save button becomes active when I enter a title
And I see x and a cancel button that I can click to exit the modal
And I can tap click outside the modal to exit the modal


Scenario 2: Create Food List at Organization Level
Given I am in the creation modal as an Organization Admin, Manager, Super Admin, or Admin
If I enter a title and hit create
Then the food list is created as an org food list
And the food list is created
And I am routed into the food list

Scenario 3: Create Food List at Location Level
Given I am in the creation modal as a Location Manager
If I enter a title and hit create
Then the food list is created as a local food list
And the food list is created
And I am routed into the food list

Title: Food List: Duplicate Food Item
Description:
As an organization user or a location user with the manage food list permission I need to be able to duplicate a food list so I can reuse already existing food list.

Portal only
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1: Duplicating a Food Item

Given I am viewing a food Item and have permission to edit it

And I am viewing the additional actions

If I click the duplicate button

Then I presented with a message saying "this food item will be duplicated"

And a title field containing (original food list item) [Copy of] that I can edit

And a confirm button

And a cancel button that closes the modal without duplicating the food item



Scenario 2: Confirming duplication of the Food Item 

Given I have clicked the duplicate button

If I press confirm 

Then the food item is duplicated with the title that is in the editable title field

And I am taken into the duplicated food item

And new food item is placed in my food list

Title: Food List: Duplicate Food List
Description: As an organization user or a location user with the manage food list permission I need to be able to duplicate a food list so I can reuse already existing food list.
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1: Duplicating a Food List

Given I am viewing a food list and have permission to edit it

And I am viewing the additional actions

If I click the duplicate button

Then I presented with a message saying "this food list will be duplicated"

And a title field containing (original food list title) [Copy of] that I can edit

And a confirm button

And a cancel button that closes the modal without deleting the food list



Scenario 2: Confirming duplication of the Food List 

Given I have clicked the duplicate button

If I press confirm 

Then the food list is duplicated with the title that is in the editable title field

And I am taken into the duplicated food list

Title: Food List: Filters & Sorts
Description:
As an organization or location user  

I want to able to filter and sort my food lists in a table 

So that I can adjust the food list I am seeing


In Scope: Portal
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1: Sort Food List Table

Given: I navigate to the food list page  
If:I sort the list of food list page
Then: I'm able to see the correct results 
  • Newest to Oldest 
  • Oldest to Newest 
  • A to Z 
  • Z to A 
Scenario 2: Filter Food Table
Given: I navigate to the food list page
If: I filter the list of food list
Then: The table returns the correct results. 
  • Owner 
    • Organization 
    • Location 
  • Date Modified  
    • Last 7 days 
    • Last 14 days 
    • Last 30 days 
    • Last 60 days 
    • Last 120 days 
Scenario 3: No Results Found
Given: I add filters and sorts
If: no data is returned 
Then: Then I see an empty state saying that my filter retuned not results

Title: Food List: Food Item - Additional Actions
Description: As an organization user or a location user with manage food list permission I need to need to be able to delete and duplicate a food item
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1: Additional Action Kabob
Given I am viewing a food item
If I have tapped the kabob on the food item
Then I see a duplicate food button
And I see a delete food button

Title: Food List: Food Items Table
Description:
As an organization or location user 

I want to create and manage food lists 

So that I can organize food items, define temperature ranges, and assign them to locations


In Scope: Portal

Design: https://www.figma.com/design/QbU1BEOWQfSc9LVrPYiJa2/Food-Lists?node-id=373-76705&m=dev
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1: Food Items Table 
Given I have navigated into a food list
If I am viewing the Food Items table
And there are foods items in the table
Then I see the tile of the food list 
And I can tap the items to enter their details
And I see a edit icon next to the title
And I see a chevron that takes me back to the food list table
And I see a food items tab with number food items in the food list
And I see a locations tab with the number of locations the food list is assigned to 
And I see a details tab
And I see search bar
And I can filter the table
  • Date Modified 
And I can sort the table
  • A to Z
  • Z to A
  • Newest to Oldest
  • Oldest to Newest
And the table is paginated  
And I see columns: 
  • Food Item Name
  • Cold Holding Compliant (e.g., "<41")
  • Hot Holding Compliant (e.g., "140>")
  • Last Modified (e.g., "01/14/25")
And I see a create food button
And I see a kabob for additional actions



Scenario 2: Food Items Table Empty State
Given I have clicked the create food list button
Or I am viewing a food list with no questions
If I see the food items table 
Then an empty state telling me there are no food items in the food list
And I see a CTA to create food

Scenario 3 : Import Foods Button
Given I am on the Food Items tab 
And I am a Super Admin
Then I see an Import Foods button next to the create food button in the top right of the screen
Or in place of the create food button in the top right during empty state
If I tap on import foods
Then a modal handles the design and UX path to import foods, as decided by the development team (Created by devs no QA needed)


Scenario 4: Location User Viewing a Organization Owned Food List
Given I am a location user with the manage food list permission 
If I am viewing a organization owned food list that has been assigned to me
Then I do not see the the create food button

Title: Food List: Food List - Additional Actions
Description: As an organization user or a location user with manage food list permission I need to need to be able to delete and duplicate a food list
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1: Additional Action Kabob
Given I am viewing a food list 
If I have tapped the kabob 
Then I see a duplicate setup button
And I see a delete setup button

Title: Food List: Navigating to Food List
Description:
As an organization or location user with the manage food list permission  

I want to see all of my food lists in a table   

So that I can organize food items, define temperature ranges, and assign them to locations 


In Scope: Portal

Design: https://www.figma.com/design/QbU1BEOWQfSc9LVrPYiJa2/Food-Lists?node-id=466-41898&m=dev
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1 : Navigating to The Food List Page
Given I am an organization or location user 
If I click food in the side nav
Then I am navigated to the food list page
And I see a list view of food list with these categories 
  • Food list title
  • # of Food Items
  • # of Locations the food list assigned to
  • Last modified date (XX/XX/XXXX)
  • Owner (Organization or Location)
And I can search the tale 
And the table is paginated 
And I see a create food list button


Scenario 2: Food List Page - Organization View
Given I am viewing the food list page 
If I am an organization user
Then I see all food list for my organization (this includes locations in my organization)
And the location owned food list are at the bottom of the list when no filters are applied 

Scenario 3: Food List Page - Location View
Given I am viewing the food list page 
If I am an location user
Then I see all food list for my organization that have been assigned to me and all of my locations food list
And all food list are mixed together

Scenario 4: Food List Table Empty State
Given I am viewing the food list page
If there are no food list in the table 
Then I see an empty telling me there are no food list created 
And I see a create food list CTA

Title: Food List: Searching Food List Table
Description:
As an organization or location user  

I want to able to search my food lists in a table 

So that I can view specific the food list I want


In Scope: Portal
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1: Searching Food List Table

Given:  I navigate to the food list Menu page   

If: I search the table based on the title

Then: My results populate as I type

And the results are shown based on the applied sorts & filters

And the table paginates based on the search results



Scenario 2: No Results
Given I am have input a search 
If no results are returned 
Then I see a an empty state telling me that no results were returned

Title: Food List: Table Pagination
Description:
As an organization or location user  

I want to see all of my food lists in a table that is paginated

So that I can adjust the amount of food list I am viewing in the table 


In Scope: Portal

Design: https://www.figma.com/design/QbU1BEOWQfSc9LVrPYiJa2/Food-Lists?node-id=466-41898&m=dev
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1: Paginating Food List Table

Given:  I navigate to the food list page  

If: I am viewing the food list table

Then: I see a paginated table 



Scenario 2: Changing the Amount of Food List in the Table

Given: I am viewing the paginated table

If: I increase the amount of items shown

Then I can choose from 

  • 25
  • 50
  • 100
  • 250 
  • 500 
And the table changes based on my selection

Title: Food Lists: Details Tab
Description:
As an organization or location user 

I want to create and manage food lists 

So that I can organize food items, define temperature ranges, and assign them to locations


In Scope: Portal
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1: View Details Tab
Given I am viewing a food list
If I click the Details tab at the top
Then I see the Food List title and subtitle (these are editable fields)
And I see the owner (organization or location with name and address)
And I see the created date
And I see the last modified
And I see the total food items
And I see the total locations assigned (if org owned and authorized role)
And I see the activity feed

Title: Food Lists: Location Tab Visibility and Navigation
Description:
As an organization or location user 

I want to create and manage food lists 

So that I can organize food items, define temperature ranges, and assign them to locations


In Scope: Portal
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1: Locations Tab for Org Owned Food List
Given I am an Org Manager, Viewer, Super Admin, or Admin viewing an org owned food list
If I click the Locations tab next to Food Items
Then I see a table of assigned locations 
And I see the location name

Scenario 2: No Locations Tab for Location Managers
Given I am a Location Manager viewing a food list
If I view the tabs in the top left if the screen
Then the Locations tab is not visible

Scenario 3: No Locations Tab for Local Food Lists
Given I am viewing a local food list
If I view the tabs in the top left if the screen
Then the Locations tab is not visible

Scenario 4: Manage Locations in Tab
Given I am in the Locations tab as a Super Admin, Admin, Org Admin, or Org Manager
If I click "Manage Locations"
Then I enter the assign data widget flow to assign locations #1021824

Title: Food Lists: Permission-Related Scenarios
Description:
As an organization or location user 

I want to create and manage food lists 

So that I can organize food items, define temperature ranges, and assign them to locations


In Scope: Portal
Acceptance Criteria:
Scenario 1: Visibility of Create Food Button
Given I am an super admin, Org admin, org manager or a location user with the manage food list permission Food Lists
Then I see the create food button
And if I do not have permission, the button is not visible

Scenario: Location Managers Editing Org Owned Lists
Given I am a location user with the manage food list permission
If I view an Org Owned Food List
Then I cannot edit it
And I do not see a created foods button
